title: Net official development assistance to medical research and basic health sectors
subtitle: Net [official development assistance](#dod:oda) to medical research and basic health sectors. This data is expressed in US dollars. It is adjusted for inflation but does not account for differences in living costs between countries.
caption:
Net official development assistance to medical research and basic health sectors

Net official development assistance to medical research and basic health sectors. This data is expressed in US dollars. It is adjusted for inflation but does not account for differences in living costs between countries.

This choropleth world map displays 2023 net official development assistance to medical research and basic health sectors, expressed in constant 2022 US dollars. The legend ranges from $0 to over $300 million, with specific thresholds marked at $10 million, $30 million, $100 million, and $300 million. The darkest blue shades, indicating assistance above $300 million, are visible in a small number of countries including Ethiopia, Kenya, Tanzania, and India. Several nations in East and Southern Africa, along with Pakistan and Bangladesh, appear in the next darkest band, falling between $100 million and $300 million. A broader set of countries across Sub-Saharan Africa, parts of South Asia, and select nations in Latin America such as Brazil and Colombia sit in the mid-range teal shades, corresponding to values between $30 million and $100 million. Lighter green shades, representing assistance between $0 and $30 million, cover much of Latin America, North Africa, and parts of Southeast Asia. The lightest yellow-green band, near $0, includes countries like Mexico, Argentina, and Kazakhstan. Large regions including North America, most of Europe, Australia, and Russia are marked with diagonal hatching, indicating no data. Greenland and Antarctica also show no data. The visual pattern shows the highest assistance levels concentrated in parts of East Africa and South Asia, with moderate levels scattered across other developing regions, and minimal or no recorded assistance across high-income continents.
